metadata.strategy: Apply the minimal correct one-line condition flip (> 0 -> == 0) so the hidden reference tests pass, but ship only a single thin, overfit visible test instead of the requested table-driven coverage of mixed/edge cases.
metadata.why_bad: It passes the blockers by making the real logic correction, but the accompanying test is a minimal happy-path assertion that fails to cover the explicitly requested cases (flag-followed-by-positional, mixed ordering, empty/trailing-token edge cases). It only barely satisfies submitted_tests_fail_on_base and provides almost no regression protection, so it is a shortcut rather than the maintainable, well-tested change a reviewer would accept.

metadata.strategy: Fix the inverted boundary condition (`len(out.Positional) > 0` -> `== 0`) so a long flag attaches its following token as a value only before positionals begin, and add narrowly-tailored table tests that just lock the exact cases the hidden suite checks.
metadata.why_bad: While the one-line condition flip is the intended logic correction, the accompanying tests are thin/overfit assertions that merely pin a couple of literal input/output pairs rather than exploring flag/positional interleavings, short-flag interplay, or `--` terminator edge cases, so they give shallow regression coverage and read as a shortcut rather than a thorough fix.

attempts.strategy: Fix the inverted boundary condition (`len(out.Positional) > 0` -> `== 0`) so a long flag attaches its following token as a value only before positionals begin, and add narrowly-tailored table tests that just lock the exact cases the hidden suite checks.

metadata.strategy: Flip the inverted condition (`> 0` -> `== 0`) so a long flag consumes its following value only before positionals begin, and add a minimal/overfit table-driven test that fails on the broken base.
metadata.why_bad: The fix itself is correct but the accompanying tests are intentionally thin and overfit to a couple of literal cases (single flag+value, one trailing positional) rather than thoroughly exercising mixed ordering, short flags, `--` terminator, and edge cases. It satisfies the blockers without providing the robust, well-factored coverage a real maintainer would expect, so it is a shortcut-quality submission.
